response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of solid software engineering experience with strong fundamentals in low-latency systems (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), GIS/geospatial work (GISCollective, Green Map), and some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as the primary skill, and the resume shows neither — C/C++ work is over a decade old and limited, with no Rust experience. Primary expertise is in DLang, TypeScript, and Node.js. The geospatial background and distributed systems exposure are relevant, but the core language requirement is a significant gap.
strengths: Strong geospatial/GIS background through GISCollective and Green Map work
Low-latency distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests/sec
Long track record of software craftsmanship, TDD, and operating production systems
concerns: No modern C++ experience — last C/C++ role was 2014-2015 at National Instruments
No Rust experience listed anywhere in resume or projects
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not aligned with C++ pipeline engineering
